title: Monday.com Export to Excel Logo
excerpt:
Hello Everyone, 
I would like to export my Monday boards into Excel files that I can send to my clients (i.e. Quotes). The issue remains that the Monday.com Logo is smack in the middle of the file which leads to confusio…

posts.author: Debbie (Deborah Funmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Everyone,
I would like to export my Monday boards into Excel files that I can send to my clients (i.e. Quotes). The issue remains that the Monday.com Logo is smack in the middle of the file which leads to confusion and skepticism from my clients.
Please does anybody have a solution to this? So that I won’t need to manually edit the header of every board I import from Monday. It would be highly appreciated. Thank you

posts.author: Mari_Fec (Mari)
posts.cookedHtml: Right click on the logo, then click Remove Link. You can then delete the logo.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Debbie:

So that I won’t need to manually edit the header of every board I import from Monday

Yes it’s possible to remove link and delete the image, but the OP doesn’t want to waste time doing that.

I agree, the monday logo in the excel export is tacky. Maybe monday needed that during the early stages to help spread their name, but at this point it just shouldn’t be there. It reminds me of “this website was built with Geocities! Click here to make your own!”

posts.author: Mari_Fec (Mari)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah, missed that, although it takes a quick second to delete. But it would certainly be better off without it.

posts.author: kmricketts (Kenicia)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is definitely a top vote for me. I am currently using Smartsheet and looking to make the switch, with Smartsheet your branding is added to PDF and Excel.

It seems easy but for larger companies having to manually remove and add your logo is a pet peeve

posts.author: Nelson_Rivera (Nelson Y Rivera)
posts.cookedHtml: Please!!! This and on the PDF prints too. Its becoming a huge issue for us.

posts.author: mdeo (Macky Deo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday. Any update on this?
I think we should have the option to not include the logo, especially those who are paying for the subscription.

And you should already have an option also to export Excel in CSV formats.

posts.author: elliek (Elham Karkouti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please remove the “power by monday” logo that is on top of every excel sheet that is created. It is frustrating having to remove it from every page.
Also, it would really help if the name of the excel sheet created matched the table used to create the sheet instead of the name of the board.
Finally, it would help if the excel worksheet included the date created.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

We hear where you’re coming from here. At this time, there is no concrete update on this particular request. We encourage you to share this feature request around to increase visibility and votes - every quarter we take the top voted feature requests to our product team for a status update. Please refer to this post to learn more about how the process works Thanks in advance for your understanding!

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Removing the logo from exports, especially for enterprise accounts, will definitely be helpful and reduce the amount of clicks we have to do, which trickles down to reducing cognitive overload.

Our finance team will be using Monday for time tracking, and they’ll need to export boards on a weekly basis. The logo doesn’t look professional and having to remove it is an additional burden on our finance team. (It was already hard to get them on board with Monday). The idea is to reduce as much administrative tasks and annoyances as possible.

posts.author: Prajwal (Prajwal Kumar)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
Also improve the Exporting, Not everyone uses Monday, Some just want reports on regular bases in a nice and clean PDF formats

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey spreadsheet fellows,

in case you are okay with using third party apps, we actually provide two solutions for this.

The first one is Exported Excel Cleaner, which still includes a bit of manual effort. But beside only removing the logo from all worksheets, it also cleans up the whole Excel file from any existing “fancy” formatting – a.k.a. “Why does my exported Excel spreadsheet look like my board?”. That means you get a plain spreadsheet with very little effort. And you can start working on it further straight away – how it’s meant to be. But the best thing is, it’s free for small teams.

The second and more mature solution is Spreadsheet Gorilla, which offers a variety of features and is therfore a bit more complex. Besides combining multiple boards (even across workspaces) in actual worksheets, you can calculate and apply Excel-like formulas just like in a spreadsheet. That being said, it also enables you to export plain spreadsheets – manually or with automations. That might be a solution for you @cphu, in case you haven’t found a proper workflow in the meantime.

Just let me know if you have questions.

– Markus from Gorilla Apps
